autoScan.label: Enable automatic scans
autoScan.help: When enabled, all configured paths will be scanned automatically at the specified interval

scanInterval.label: Scan interval (hours)
scanInterval.help: How often the automatic scan should run (minimum 1 hour)

daysUntilStale.label: Days until 'stale' (warning)
daysUntilStale.help: Datapoints not updated for this many days are marked as 'stale'

daysUntilDead.label: Days until 'dead' (critical)
daysUntilDead.help: Datapoints not updated for this many days are marked as 'dead'

_pathsInfo.text: Configure which paths to scan for unused datapoints. Each path will get its own result state with a JSON table.

items.title: Check alias targets
items.tooltip: For alias.* paths: Check if target datapoints exist (ghost detection)

_fieldInfo.text: Each scan result contains a JSON array with the following 10 fields per datapoint:

_field_name.text:
2  -  name
common.name or last part of ID (user-friendly name)

_field_last_ts.text:
3  -  last_ts
Unix timestamp in ms or null (for sorting)

_field_last_ts_iso.text:
4  -  last_ts_iso
Formatted date string (for display in table)

_field_value.text:
5  -  value
Current datapoint value (final check before deletion)

_field_status.text:
6  -  status
Classification in English: active, dead, stale, undefined, orphaned

_field_status_de.text:
7  -  status_de
Classification in German: aktiv, inaktiv, veraltet, undefiniert, verwaist

_field_issue.text:
8  -  issue
Problem indicator (English): dead, stale, orphaned_alias, or null if OK

_field_issue_de.text:
9  -  issue_de
Problem indicator (German): inaktiv, veraltet, verwaistes Alias, or null if OK

_usageInfo.text:
For German tables: Use status_de and issue_de columns
For English tables: Use status and issue columns

Filter by 'issue != null' to show only problematic datapoints.
